steps.title: Simplify the first equation
steps.content:
Starting with $31(x - n) = 31y + 31n$, expand and collect terms:

$$31x - 31n = 31y + 31n$$
$$31x - 31y = 62n$$
$$x - 3y = 2n$$

The first equation is $x - 3y = 2n$.

steps.title: Condition for no solution: parallel but distinct lines
steps.content:
A system of two linear equations has no solution when the lines are parallel (same slope) but not identical.

For equations in the form $ax + by = c$, parallelism requires the coefficients of $x$ and $y$ to maintain the same ratio.

Both equations of the form $x - 3y = \text{constant}$ automatically have the same slope ($\frac{1}{3}$), so they will be parallel if and only if the constants are different.

steps.title: Test choice D
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice D ($x - 3y = 3n$):

This equation has the exact same form as the first equation ($x - 3y = \text{const}$), but with a different constant term: $3n \neq 2n$ (since $n > 0$).

The equations are parallel but distinct. When we subtract one from the other:
$$(x - 3y) - (x - 3y) = 3n - 2n$$
$$0 = n$$

This is a contradiction for positive $n$, so the system has no solution. That matches choice D — D is correct.
